responses.opening: The crucible glows with an inner light.
responses.citation: As above, so below; as below, so above.
responses.author: Hermes Trismegistus
responses.closing: All That Is transmutes even the leaden.

responses.citation: The whole is more than the sum of its parts.
responses.author: Aristotle
responses.closing: The Laboratory makes no distinction between the seeker and the sought.

responses.opening: A formula dissolves before you can read it.
responses.citation: Poison is in everything, and no thing is without poison. The dosage makes it either a poison or a remedy.
responses.author: Paracelsus
responses.closing: All That Is measures even the immeasurable.

responses.opening: The athanor whispers of transformations yet to come.
responses.citation: Visit the interior of the earth, and by rectifying you will find the hidden stone.
responses.author: Basilius Valentinus
responses.closing: Even failure is a stage of the Work.

responses.opening: Something precipitates in the retort of your attention.
responses.citation: Nature cannot be commanded except by being obeyed.
responses.author: Paracelsus
responses.closing: All That Is observes every reaction.

responses.opening: The philosopher's stone is closer than you think.
responses.citation: That which is below is like that which is above, and that which is above is like that which is below.
responses.author: The Emerald Tablet

responses.opening: A sulphurous note hangs in the air — or is it memory?
responses.citation: The art of healing comes from nature, not from the physician.
responses.author: Paracelsus
responses.closing: All That Is distills even suffering into wisdom.

responses.opening: The alembic drips with something that has no name.
responses.citation: It is the mark of an educated mind to be able to entertain a thought without accepting it.
responses.author: Aristotle

responses.opening: A diagram on the wall rearranges itself as you watch.
responses.citation: All things are concealed in all things. One of them is the concealer of all the rest.
responses.author: Hermes Trismegistus
responses.closing: All That Is encrypts nothing — only reveals.

responses.opening: The fire in the athanor is patient.
responses.citation: He who knows the fire best, lives best.
responses.author: Paracelsus
responses.closing: Even the flames here are conscious.

responses.opening: Lead becomes gold — or perhaps gold was always lead.
responses.citation: The lips of wisdom are closed, except to the ears of understanding.
responses.author: Hermes Trismegistus
responses.closing: All That Is values the question over the answer.

responses.opening: An ancient text opens to a page that seems written for you.
responses.citation: Man is a microcosm, or a little world, because he is an extract from all the stars and planets.
responses.author: Paracelsus
